honglee.description:
Frontend developer with 3 years of experience based in Seoul, with a passion for crafting beautiful interfaces and animations.

I believe a developer's value comes from improving lives through better technology. At Daum — one of Korea's largest search platforms under Kakao — I focused on UI consistency, search flow optimization, and accurate information delivery.

Having worked across design, product, and development, I understand the importance of collaboration.

design.description: Built a design system that auto-converts Figma design tokens to CSS variables for theme consistency. Documented components with Storybook and automated separate static/dynamic deployments using GitHub Actions in a monorepo.

daypalette.description: A UI dashboard that visualizes daily weather and air quality data as gradient color values

timequestion.description: A time-based interactive UI with hourly state management, per-minute updates, and random content logic

alphabet.description: A word-building web interface combining drag & drop, gravity physics, and real-time translation

kiosk.title: Ministry of Justice Kiosk & Webtoon Viewer Web Service (Gwangju)
items: Solely handled planning, design, and frontend development for the exhibition hall information kiosk system
Built a React SPA to improve screen transitions and content navigation experience
Integrated building maps, facility guides, catalogs, and a webtoon viewer into a single web service

yoga.title: WordPress-based Global Yoga Studio Website
items: Led frontend development of a multilingual yoga studio site using WordPress CMS
Implemented reservations, membership management, and payment modules via plugin customization

cafe24.title: Cafe24 & Gnuboard-based Online Shop Development
items: Analyzed legacy PHP CMS template structure and improved product detail, cart, and checkout UI
Implemented dynamic product filtering and real-time inventory display using jQuery
cafe24.stack: Tech Stack: React · JavaScript (ES6+) · HTML/CSS · jQuery · WordPress · PHP

daum.title: Daum Search Collection Frontend Development
items: Developed and improved UIs for various collections: places, stocks, world clock, weather, and more
Collaborated with product, design, and backend teams to coordinate UI decisions around constraints, accessibility, and data structure
Ensured UI consistency through testing across 40+ device and browser environments

election.title: 2024 22nd National Assembly Election Collection
items: Developed a polling station search UI for a seasonal collection, prioritizing speed and accuracy during the election period
Implemented lightweight UI by replacing only the list area with AJAX rendering on keyword or location-based requests
Blocked client-side security threats (parameter tampering, XSS) via regex-based input filtering
Collaborated with Kakao Maps to align search radius, sorting, and keyword interpretation — achieved ~97% result accuracy

storybook.title: Search UI Migration to Storybook-based Architecture
items: Gradually migrated from Freemarker to a React + Storybook component structure
Established UI composition principles with product and design teams; componentized shared elements for reuse
Improved QA and design communication efficiency by ~1.5× through Storybook visualization
Directly experienced the transition to a Node-based, frontend-driven build and deployment pipeline

realestate.title: Real Estate Collection: New Features & Improvements
items: Built a 3-level regional dropdown filter with query-based refresh for search engine indexing optimization
Improved comparative browsing by visualizing transaction type data as separate categories
Implemented Flipsnap slides, area unit conversion, and Kakao sharing with jQuery
Shifted from a backend-first workflow to a frontend-first mock-data structure, improving speed and collaboration
realestate.stack: Tech Stack: JavaScript · TypeScript · React · jQuery · Freemarker · SCSS · Storybook

designSystem.title: Design System UI Components & Deployment Automation
items: Implemented React + Storybook components based on Figma design tokens
Automated separate static (Storybook) and dynamic (Next.js) deployments with GitHub Actions
Managed project as a monorepo, improving maintainability and deployment efficiency
designSystem.stack: Tech Stack: Next.js · React · TypeScript · Emotion · Storybook · Figma · GitHub Actions · Vercel · pnpm

portfolio.title: Interaction-based Portfolio Projects
items: Built interaction-focused side projects using Framer Motion, Matter.js, and dnd-kit
Alphabet Builder: word-building game with drag-and-drop and a physics engine
Day Palette: color gradient UI visualization based on real-time weather data
Time Question: minute-by-minute rotating question prompts based on the current time
portfolio.stack: Tech Stack: React · TypeScript · Emotion · Framer Motion · Matter.js · dnd-kit

paper.title: Paper: "Responsive Web Design Development of Digital Fashion Platform Musinsa"
items: Documented the process of redesigning an adaptive web structure into a fully responsive one
Proposed UI/UX improvements considering diverse screen sizes and user behavior patterns

story.title: Accessibility as Default
story.body: During university, I worked part-time at an exhibition run by visually impaired curators. For the first time, I learned that visual impairment spans a wide spectrum — total blindness, low vision, color vision deficiency, tunnel vision — and that each person experiences the world differently. Yet one thing they all had in common: reliance on sound to access digital devices. Now as a frontend developer, that experience reminds me that accessibility isn't a special feature for a few — it's a fundamental design principle for everyone.

why.title: Why Should Frontend Developers Care About Accessibility?
items: Equal access for all users — As of 2024, registered persons with disabilities in Korea number approximately 2.63 million (5.1% of the population). Accessibility is a baseline requirement, not an optional add-on
Business value — Inclusive products reach wider markets and build long-term brand trust and competitive advantage
Quality and efficiency — Building with semantic structure, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support from the start reduces long-term maintenance costs significantly
Shared team culture — When accessibility is baked in as a default, designers, developers, QA, and PMs all naturally uphold those standards together

carbon.title: IBM Carbon Design System
carbon.caption1: Focus Management and Keyboard Interaction documented per component
items: Every component explicitly documents Focus Management, Keyboard Interaction, and Screen Reader Behavior
Combines automated checks (DAP, axe-core) with manual QA (keyboard and screen reader testing) across the full component lifecycle
One of the most thorough accessibility implementations among major design systems

gestalt.title: Pinterest Gestalt
gestalt.caption: Accessibility props like accessibilityLabel built into the component interface
items: Props like accessibilityLabel and accessibilityControls are part of the default component API
useFocusVisible — shows focus ring only on keyboard navigation, hides it on mouse click
useReducedMotion — detects the user's 'reduce motion' system setting and adjusts animations automatically

primer.title: GitHub Primer
primer.caption: Dedicated Accessibility tab on every component page
items: Every component has a dedicated Accessibility tab with explicit requirements
Includes implementation notes, best practices, and screen reader guidance directly in the component docs

voice.title: Voice Feedback Design
items: The SpeechSynthesis API enables context-aware audio guidance beyond what a screen reader provides by default
Rather than simply announcing 'Transfer complete,' contextual voice feedback can communicate remaining balance and the next available action — helping users navigate financial flows naturally
By controlling what gets read and in what order, developers can shape a coherent, service-specific audio experience

haptic.title: Haptic Interaction Design
items: The Vibration API allows haptic feedback on key events like button taps or swipe completions
It communicates successful interactions through touch — crucial when visual feedback may not be perceived
A short vibration on swipe-to-delete, or a subtle pulse on payment completion, helps users feel that something happened

ide.title: IDE Level — Accessibility Linting
items: Catch accessibility errors at the point of writing code with an ESLint-based a11y plugin
Get immediate warnings for issues like missing aria-label on buttons or incorrect semantic structures
The fastest feedback loop — surface issues developers might not notice before they become habits

ci.title: PR/Build Stage — Automated Checks
items: Integrate axe-core automated scripts into the CI/CD pipeline to catch accessibility violations before merging
Fail PRs when violations are detected, enforcing quality at the development stage
Creates a team-wide culture where accessibility compliance is structurally enforced, not individually remembered

storybook.title: Design System — Storybook a11y
items: Use the Storybook a11y addon to visually verify color contrast, semantic errors, and missing aria attributes during component development
Catching issues at component level prevents compound problems from surfacing at the page level
Serves as a shared visual reference for accessibility standards between designers and developers

manual.title: Manual Testing — VoiceOver & Keyboard
items: Verify that every interactive element is reachable via Tab key alone, and that focus-visible styles are clearly visible
Use VoiceOver (macOS/iOS) or TalkBack (Android) to experience the screen reader perspective firsthand
Automated tools catch roughly 30–40% of accessibility issues — manual testing is essential to cover the rest

audit.title: Open Source — a11y-audit
items: Built a Node.js CLI tool that opens a URL with Playwright and runs axe-core accessibility checks
Returns exit code 1 on critical/serious violations — automatically fails PRs in CI/CD pipelines
Integrated into this portfolio via GitHub Actions — every PR triggers an accessibility audit with results posted as a comment
Supports --format markdown for outputting reports ready to paste directly into GitHub PR comments

toss.title: Testing Toss App with VoiceOver
items: Discovered duplicate narration — some content was read twice consecutively
The < > buttons (prev/next month) were announced only as 'button' with no context — missing aria-label
Important safety notices provided as images without alt text — content completely inaccessible to screen reader users
Canvas-based interactions like scratch cards and signature fields were entirely unrecognized by VoiceOver — Canvas accessibility requires explicit ARIA handling
Percentage values were read out to 8 decimal places, making the information harder to process rather than easier
This hands-on test reinforced that using a screen reader yourself is the fastest way to develop real accessibility intuition as a developer
